inclusion_criteria: Comparative human studies (RCTs or observational cohorts) of adults with primary FIGO stage IV endometrial carcinoma undergoing cytoreductive surgery that report outcomes by residual disease status (complete/optimal vs incomplete/non-optimal, as defined by the study).
Report overall survival (e.g., hazard ratios, adjusted HRs, or median OS with comparative statistics) comparing complete/optimal to incomplete cytoreduction.
Provide stage IV–specific results; mixed-stage cohorts are eligible only if data for primary stage IV patients are separately extractable.
For overlapping datasets, include the most complete, non-duplicate report.
exclusion_criteria: Single-arm series, case reports, or studies without a comparator based on completeness of cytoreduction; studies comparing surgery vs no surgery without residual disease stratification.
Studies focused on recurrent disease, secondary cytoreduction, or non–stage IV populations without a separable stage IV subgroup.
No overall survival data or no survival reported according to residual disease completeness.
Non-endometrial uterine malignancies (e.g., uterine sarcoma) or other gynecologic cancers; studies of purely palliative procedures without cytoreductive intent.
screening_criteria.description: Screening focuses on comparative studies in primary FIGO stage IV endometrial cancer that contrast complete/optimal versus incomplete cytoreductive surgery and report overall survival. Author definitions of completeness/optimality are accepted; mixed-stage cohorts must provide separable stage IV data.

included_articles.title: The role of surgical cytoreduction in Stage IV endometrial carcinoma.
Stage IVB endometrial carcinoma: the role of cytoreductive surgery and determinants of survival.
Optimal cytoreduction followed by chemoradiation in stage IVB uterine serous carcinoma.
Clinicopathological prognostic factors and the role of cytoreduction in surgical stage IVb endometrial cancer: a retrospective multi-institutional analysis of 248 patients in Japan.
Surgical cytoreduction in stage IV endometrioid endometrial carcinoma.
The role of cytoreductive surgery in the management of stage IV uterine papillary serous carcinoma.
The role of optimal debulking in advanced stage serous carcinoma of the uterus.
